'''Eidolon Base''' was the name used by [[Marl Semtin/Legends|Marl Semtin]] to indicate the location of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Eidolon (Strike-class)|''Eidolon'']] and its weapons cache on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].

==History==
The base was located along the edge of the [[Northern Dune Sea/Legends|Northern Dune Sea]]. It was the property of [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], a high-ranking [[Galactic Empire/Legends|imperial]] official who lived in fear of being purged. He was put in charge of constructing the [[Eidolon (Strike-class)|''Eidolon'']], an experimental strike cruiser to advance the Imperial [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war effort]]. Due to it being "barely space worthy" and the [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|advisor's]] paranoia, Pestage took and hid the ship for himself without his [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|superiors']] knowledge. He then used the continuing project funding to build the installation and purchase munitions, art and other forms of wealth from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="X12" />

Pestage entrusted [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Marl Semtin/Legends|Marl Semtin]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] to guard the secret location. The captain failed when the base was [[Tatooine Campaign|discovered]] by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and seized from Imperial control. Later, after it was emptied by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], a group of former Imperials took control of the base and used it as a shelter for Imperial supporters unwilling to trasverse to the remote systems of the [[Imperial Remnant]].<ref name="X12" /> Over the years, the New Republic ceded the land on which the base was located to the Imperials, and Eidolon Base became a wealthy city unto itself. By [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], the leader of Eidolon Base was [[Arajen Turmen]].<ref name="SOT">''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]''</ref>
